mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, increasing equity present values and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns following low FEDFUNDS periods fail to exceed the baseline positive rate of 65.5% over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding ten percent signals severe aggregate demand destruction and falling corporate earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than fifty percent of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds ten percent, or the mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the unemployment rate and declines in industrial production indicate a broad-based recessionary contraction across services and manufacturing sectors; this joint deterioration amplifies downside earnings risk beyond single-series effects and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE rose to 14.7% and INDPRO fell to 92.59 in April 2020
failure_condition: Negative returns occur in fewer than forty percent of sixty-day periods where UNRATE rises and INDPRO falls concurrently, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power above main effects (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, a month-over-month increase in housing starts signals renewed residential investment and construction employment that leads broader cyclical equity sectors, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value 1216 thousand units in March 2020
failure_condition: Positive returns follow month-over-month HOUST increases in less than fifty-five percent of post-shock recovery months, or the signal fails to outperform baseline buy-and-hold (Sharpe ratio lower).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production following three consecutive monthly declines signals manufacturing stabilization and inventory restocking that triggers a short-term positive repricing of cyclical equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises after three consecutive monthly declines
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 92.5919 in April 2020 after prior declines
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns are negative in more than fifty percent of trigger events, or the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate from near-zero levels signals premature monetary tightening that shocks risk asset valuations through higher discount rates and reduced liquidity, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next five trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.05% as of April 2020 near the zero lower bound
failure_condition: Negative returns follow month-over-month FEDFUNDS increases in fewer than forty percent of instances, or positive returns dominate with a win rate exceeding sixty percent.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding corporate revenue bases and sustained consumer spending power, which drives broad-based equity appreciation and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value 131045 thousand in April 2020
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in less than fifty-five percent of months when PAYEMS increases, or the average sixty-day return is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in consumer price inflation and rising unemployment creates a stagflationary environment that compresses corporate profit margins and raises real discount rates; this interaction generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 3% while UNRATE exceeds 6%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 255.902 and UNRATE at 14.7% as of April 2020
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than fifty percent of triggered stagflationary months, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).
